meta.name: ACE Adverse Childhood Experiences Questionnaire
meta.description: The ACE Questionnaire is a 10-item screening tool that assesses adverse childhood experiences before age 18. It covers abuse (physical, emotional, sexual), neglect (physical, emotional), and household dysfunction (domestic violence, substance abuse, mental illness, parental separation, incarceration). Higher ACE scores are associated with increased risk of health problems in adulthood.
meta.author: Felitti et al. (1998)
meta.reference: Felitti VJ, Anda RF, Nordenberg D, et al. Relationship of childhood abuse and household dysfunction to many of the leading causes of death in adults: the Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACE) Study. Am J Prev Med. 1998;14(4):245-258.
meta.instruction: Prior to your 18th birthday, did any of the following happen to you? Please answer each question honestly.

questions.text: Did a parent or other adult in the household often swear at you, insult you, put you down, or humiliate you, or act in a way that made you afraid that you might be physically hurt?
Did a parent or other adult in the household often push, grab, slap, or throw something at you, or ever hit you so hard that you had marks or were injured?
Did an adult or person at least 5 years older than you ever touch or fondle you or have you touch their body in a sexual way, or attempt or actually have oral, anal, or vaginal intercourse with you?
Did you often feel that no one in your family loved you or thought you were important or special, or your family didn't look out for each other, feel close to each other, or support each other?

Did you often feel that you didn't have enough to eat, had to wear dirty clothes, and had no one to protect you, or your parents were too drunk or high to take care of you or take you to the doctor if you needed it?
Were your parents ever separated or divorced?

Was your mother or stepmother often pushed, grabbed, slapped, or had something thrown at her, or sometimes or often kicked, bitten, hit with a fist, or hit with something hard, or ever repeatedly hit or threatened with a knife or gun?
Did you live with anyone who was a problem drinker or alcoholic, or who used street drugs?
Was a household member depressed or mentally ill, or did a household member attempt suicide?
Did a household member go to prison?

ranges.label: No Adverse Experiences
ranges.description: You reported no adverse childhood experiences. This is a protective factor for long-term health.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy relationships and lifestyle
If you care about others' childhood experiences, learn how to provide support

ranges.label: Low Risk
ranges.description: You reported 1 adverse childhood experience. Research shows even a small number of adverse experiences can have some impact on health.
suggestions: Learn about how childhood experiences can affect adult health
Develop healthy coping strategies and support networks
Consider talking to a professional if needed

ranges.label: Moderate Risk
ranges.description: You reported 2-3 adverse childhood experiences. The cumulative effect of multiple adverse experiences may increase health risks.
suggestions: Pay attention to your mental health
Learn stress management and emotion regulation techniques
Consider seeking counseling to process past experiences
Build a strong social support network

ranges.label: High Risk
ranges.description: You reported 4 or more adverse childhood experiences. High ACE scores are significantly associated with increased risk of multiple physical and mental health problems in adulthood.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional psychological help
Trauma-informed therapy can effectively help process childhood trauma
Monitor your physical and mental health; get regular check-ups
Do not carry the burden alone; share your experiences with trusted people
Remember: ACE scores are not destiny — with appropriate support and intervention, recovery is possible
